prototype_prefill_shape_check.output_summary: All four ranks printed ok=True at iteration 9 for 512 token rows with three reused slots.

vllm_smokes.name: eager_ipc_liveness
vllm_smokes.evidence: Rank 0 logged MiniMax Q/K XPU IPC allreduce initialized (world_size=4, slots=128, max_tokens=1024).
vllm_smokes.interpretation: Correct liveness smoke only; eager mode is not a performance benchmark.

vllm_smokes.name: compiled_ipc_attempt
vllm_smokes.symptom: TorchDynamo rejected the disabled ensure_initialized function inside the compiled forward path.
vllm_smokes.interpretation: IPC mailbox setup must move outside the compiled region before this can be a real speed path.

vllm_smokes.name: compiled_flag_fallback_after_guard
vllm_smokes.interpretation: The guard now avoids a compiled-mode crash by falling back to oneCCL; this is not an IPC performance result.

vllm_smokes.name: compiled_ipc_preinit_opt_in_counter_path
vllm_smokes.symptom: IPC preinitialized and the compiled path entered the custom op, but p1/n4 generation took hundreds of seconds and the wrapper timed out before writing JSON.
vllm_smokes.interpretation: Moving initialization before graph capture solves the Dynamo rejection but does not make the Python-level custom op placement a speed path.

vllm_smokes.name: compiled_preinit_flag_fallback
vllm_smokes.interpretation: The default compiled guard keeps real benchmarks on oneCCL unless VLLM_MINIMAX_QK_RMS_XPU_IPC_COMPILED=1 is explicitly set.

vllm_smokes.name: eager_scalar_sequence_ipc
vllm_smokes.evidence: Rank 0 logged MiniMax Q/K XPU IPC allreduce initialized, then vLLM logged shared-memory wait warnings before completion.
vllm_smokes.interpretation: The scalar sequence protocol passes standalone but desynchronizes or fences badly under vLLM scheduling.

vllm_smokes.name: compiled_scalar_sequence_ipc_opt_in
vllm_smokes.interpretation: Compiling does not rescue the scalar IPC path; keep VLLM_MINIMAX_QK_RMS_XPU_IPC_SCALAR_SEQ unset.

vllm_smokes.name: standalone_device_counter_decode_microbench
vllm_smokes.comparison: Earlier XCCL microbenchmark measured about 0.016 ms for a decode-sized tiny allreduce.
vllm_smokes.interpretation: The mailbox primitive itself is too slow; vLLM slowdown is not merely Python scheduling overhead.

decision: Keep all VLLM_MINIMAX_QK_RMS_XPU_IPC flags unset for throughput benchmarks. Pre-initialization fixes the compile-time setup crash, but the current mailbox allreduce is hundreds of milliseconds for a one-token [1,2] payload. Next work should avoid standalone IPC allreduce and instead target larger local fusions such as Q/K RMS apply plus RoPE/KV cache update, or an XPU equivalent of CUDA minimax_allreduce_rms_qk only if peer reads are amortized inside a much larger kernel.
